data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices architecture to support scalability and manageability of its massive volume of data and user interactions.
data.technical_spec: Microservices written in multiple languages such as Java, C++, and Python, deployed via containers on Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Global CDN ensures fast and reliable video streaming service to users worldwide by caching content close to users' geographic locations.
data.technical_spec: Uses partners and proprietary solutions to cache static content, ensuring low latency across diverse regions.

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Distributed network of data centers supports global infrastructure demands.
data.technical_spec: High availability with state-of-the-art server management and optimized energy use.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Dynamic load balancing manages the distribution of traffic across servers to prevent overload.
data.technical_spec: Employs algorithms to predictably distribute load based on traffic patterns.

data.title: Video Transcoding
data.description: Videos are transcoded into various resolutions and formats for compatibility with diverse devices.
data.technical_spec: Automated process that optimizes for device-specific playback requirements.
